channel.title: Awesome Search KG
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Awesome Search KG

item.title: Doug Turnbull
item.description: Doug Turnbull Co-founder of OpenSource Connections (OSC); co-author of Relevant Search (O’Reilly).

item.title: Presentation Bias
item.description: Presentation Bias Definition The distortion in click-based relevance signals caused by the fact that users can only interact with results that are actually displayed to them.

item.title: Relevance Feedback
item.description: Relevance Feedback Definition Using signals about which results are relevant (or not) to iteratively refine a search query and improve subsequent retrieval.

item.title: Listwise Relevance Evaluation
item.description: Listwise Relevance Evaluation Definition A relevance judgment method where an evaluator receives a full list of candidates for a query and produces a ranked ordering or set of grades over all items simultaneously.

item.title: Pairwise Relevance Evaluation
item.description: Pairwise Relevance Evaluation Definition A relevance judgment method where an evaluator (human or LLM) is shown two candidates side-by-side — conventionally called LHS (left-hand side) and RHS (right-hand side) — and asked which is more relevant to a given query.

item.title: Pointwise Relevance Evaluation
item.description: Pointwise Relevance Evaluation Definition A relevance judgment method where each (query, document) pair is scored independently, without reference to other candidates.

item.title: Classic ML to Cope with Dumb LLM Judges
item.description: Classic ML to Cope with Dumb LLM Judges Doug Turnbull shows how to combine many “dumb” single-attribute LLM judges into a smarter relevance prediction by treating their outputs as features for a classical ML decision tree classifier.

item.title: Search Quality Assurance with AI as a Judge
item.description: Search Quality Assurance with AI as a Judge Tao Ruangyam (Zalando) describes how Zalando built a production LLM-as-a-judge pipeline to evaluate search quality at scale — with high coverage, multi-language support, and no hand-crafted test cases.

item.title: This Is What Agentic Retrieval Looks Like
item.description: This Is What Agentic Retrieval Looks Like Jo Kristian Bergum (Hornet) analyzes 19,279 search calls made by GPT-5 during BrowseComp-Plus benchmark runs to characterize the agentic query workload.

item.title: What AI Engineers Should Know about Search
item.description: What AI Engineers Should Know about Search Doug Turnbull provides a 58-point primer for AI engineers entering the search domain — covering lexical search fundamentals (BM25, tokenization, phrase search), relevance evaluation and judgment methodology, query understanding pipelines, and Learning to Ra...
